bonuses.leadin: Much of the literature circulated in this manner was written on East German Erika typewriters, as the Soviet Union kept typographic samples of every individual domestic printing device. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this form of dissident literature in the Eastern bloc in which illegal manuscripts passed from person to person. Doctor Zhivago was the first full-length novel to circulate in this form.

This Mikhail Bulgakov novel circulated for decades as samizdat before a complete version was published in 1973. This novel, in which the devil visits Moscow, is the source of the quote “manuscripts don’t burn.”

This Hungarian author of Abigél spent the 1950s teaching at a girl’s school after being labeled an enemy of the party. Bernard Rix’s translation of this author’s novel The Door was republished in 2015 to widespread acclaim.
answers_sanitized: The Master and Margarita [or Мастер и Маргарита]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Much of the literature circulated in this manner was written on East German Erika typewriters, as the Soviet Union kept typographic samples of every individual domestic printing device. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this form of dissident literature in the Eastern bloc in which illegal manuscripts passed from person to person. Doctor Zhivago was the first full-length novel to circulate in this form.

This Mikhail Bulgakov novel circulated for decades as samizdat before a complete version was published in 1973. This novel, in which the devil visits Moscow, is the source of the quote "manuscripts don't burn."

This Hungarian author of Abigel spent the 1950s teaching at a girl's school after being labeled an enemy of the party. Bernard Rix's translation of this author's novel The Door was republished in 2015 to widespread acclaim.

bonuses.leadin: Zimbabwean translator Len Rix won the 2018 PEN Translation prize for his translation of this novel. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel in which a group of children play the game Cherry Tree before the onset of World War II. Later in life, Irén and Bálint marry, while Henriette Weld, who was shot by invading Nazis, occupies a shadowy afterlife and observes her friends as a ghost.
This author of Katalin Street wrote a cheerier young adult adventure novel, Abigail, and a novel about a writer’s complex friendship with her unconventional housekeeper Emerence, The Door.
Magda Szabó, like László Krasznahorkai (“KRASS-nuh-HOR-kye”) and Fatelessness author Imre Kertész (“KER-tayss”), wrote primarily in this European language.
